summary: Indian passport holders require a visa to enter the UAE. Tourist visas can be applied for through airlines (Emirates, Etihad, flydubai), authorized travel agencies, or UAE-based sponsors. The 30-day tourist visa is the most common. If you hold a valid US visa, UK visa, EU residence permit, or Green Card, you may be eligible for a visa on arrival for 14 days.
requirements: Hotel booking confirmation or host's details
Bank statement showing sufficient funds (minimum AED 5,000 recommended)
NOC from employer (if employed in India)
steps: Choose your application channel: airline, travel agency, or UAE sponsor
If applying through airline: book a flight on Emirates/Etihad/flydubai and apply via their website
If applying through agency: submit documents to an authorized UAE visa agency
Receive visa via email (usually 3-5 business days)
Print the visa approval or save electronically
Present at immigration upon arrival in UAE
tips: Emirates airline offers complimentary visa processing for business/first class passengers
If you have a valid US visa/Green Card, you can get 14-day visa on arrival
Overstaying your visa carries heavy fines (AED 200/day)
The 90-day multiple entry visa is better value if planning multiple visits
Apply through official channels only — many scam agencies operate online
commonReasons: Transit (Dubai is a major global hub)
Visiting family (large Indian diaspora in UAE)

embassyInfo.name: Embassy of UAE in New Delhi
embassyInfo.address: EP-12, Chandragupta Marg, Chanakyapuri, New Delhi 110021

faqs.question: Can I get a UAE visa on arrival as an Indian passport holder?
faqs.answer: Only if you hold a valid US visa, US Green Card, UK residence visa, or EU residence permit. In that case, you can get a 14-day visa on arrival. Otherwise, you must obtain a visa before travel.

faqs.question: How much money do I need to show for a UAE tourist visa?
faqs.answer: While there's no official minimum, having at least AED 5,000-10,000 (USD $1,360-$2,720) in your bank account for the last 3 months is recommended to demonstrate financial capability.

faqs.question: Can I extend my UAE tourist visa?
faqs.answer: Yes, 30-day tourist visas can be extended once for an additional 30 days. You must apply for extension before the original visa expires through GDRFA or an authorized typing center.

faqs.question: Is Dubai transit visa free for Indians?
faqs.answer: If you're transiting through Dubai for less than 8 hours and don't leave the airport, you don't need a visa. For longer transits or if you want to exit the airport, you'll need a transit visa or tourist visa.

seo.title: India to UAE Visa 2026 | Dubai Visa for Indian Passport Holders
seo.description: Complete UAE visa guide for Indian citizens. Tourist visa requirements, fees, processing time, visa on arrival eligibility, and step-by-step application process for 2026.
